---
title: "Tracking Progress"
format: html
---

## Tracking Progress

It is important for both you and your counselor to know where you are in the recovery process at all times. Marking progress helps in several ways:

-   It reminds you of how far you’ve come.

-   It can give you a sense of pride when you see the number of days you have been abstinent.

-   Recovery can feel very long unless you measure progress in smaller, more manageable steps.

![](https://github.com/jjcurtin/aud_support/blob/main/modules/images/growth.png?raw=true){fig-align="center"}

### Instructions

1.  Use the calendar below (or your own calendar) to mark each day of abstinence.

2.  Choose a symbol (✔, ✪, X, or your own mark) to use consistently.

3.  Review your progress with your counselor (or a supportive friend or sponsor) regularly.

4.  Continue this exercise to stay motivated and track your long-term progress.

### My Abstinence Calendar

Month: \_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_ Year: \_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_

| Sun | Mon | Tue | Wed | Thu | Fri | Sat |
|-----|-----|-----|-----|-----|-----|-----|
|     |     |     |     |     |     |     |
|     |     |     |     |     |     |     |
|     |     |     |     |     |     |     |
|     |     |     |     |     |     |     |
|     |     |     |     |     |     |     |

*(Make a mark each day you are abstinent)*
